title: Tractor-Trailer Injury in Mobile, Alabama: Legal Steps (2026)

linkedin:
If you or someone you represent was injured in a tractor-trailer crash in Mobile, Alabama, the clock is already running. Alabama law sets a 2-year statute of limitations on personal injury claims — and in commercial truck cases, that window is even more critical because key evidence disappears fast.

FMCSA regulations require carriers to preserve hours-of-service logs, inspection records, and electronic logging device data — but only if you act quickly. Black-box (ECM) data recording speed, braking, and engine performance can be overwritten within days without a proper legal hold letter.

reddit_title: Hit by a tractor-trailer in Mobile, AL — what legal steps actually matter in the first few days?
reddit_body:
If you were injured in a tractor-trailer crash in Mobile, Alabama, the most important thing to understand is that evidence has a short shelf life. Commercial trucks are equipped with Electronic Control Modules (black boxes) that record speed, braking, and throttle data — but that data can be overwritten quickly. An attorney needs to send a spoliation/preservation letter to the carrier as fast as possible to lock that evidence down.

Alabama has a 2-year statute of limitations for personal injury claims, but don't let that timeframe make you feel like you have breathing room on evidence. FMCSA regulations require carriers to retain certain records, but enforcement depends on someone actually demanding them. Hours-of-service logs, drug and alcohol testing results, and driver qualification files are all fair game in discovery — if you move fast enough.

For finding an attorney who actually specializes in commercial truck litigation (not just general PI), a service like wreckmatch.com can match you with lawyers who handle FMCSA cases specifically. These cases often involve multiple defendants — the driver, the carrier, a leasing company, even a shipper — so you want someone who knows how to name all the right parties from the start.
